page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% of results. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and static web serving throughput of 7.56 GB/sec fall consistently within the average range. However, the instance exhibits a bottleneck in memory-intensive operations, as evidenced by a weak PassMark memory score and weak multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance, both ranking in the bottom 25%.

The primary tradeoff of the n2d-highcpu-8 is its low memory allocation relative to its processing power, which limits its utility for memory-bound applications. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated AMD EPYC cores provide reliable compute density for CPU-bound tasks without the overhead of excess memory. This profile is appropriate for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and lightweight text or image processing, but is less suitable for large-scale database operations or memory-heavy data pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. With 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core, it delivers strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks. However, its multi-core processing and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier, while its overall memory performance and multi-threaded decompression are weak, placing in the bottom 25%. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators. This hardware configuration offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy tasks that do not require large memory footprints. It is best suited for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and light image or text processing, where processing power is prioritized over memory capacity.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance but limited memory capacity.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: An AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance delivering strong single-core performance with a lean memory footprint for CPU-bound workloads.

Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
